data.title: Rogers: Capex Cuts, Strong Q1, and Growing Sports & Media Assets Drive Upgraded Buy Thesis
data.description:
BMO Capital analyst Tim Casey has maintained their bullish stance on RCI stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tim Casey has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including a sizable cut to Rogers’ 2026 capital spending plan, which materially lifts projected free cash flow while keeping revenue and EBITDA growth targets intact. He highlights that the lower, sustained capital intensity should support faster balance sheet deleveraging over the next several years, enhancing equity value despite a slower-growth industry backdrop.

He also points to a solid first quarter, with revenue, EBITDA, and free cash flow all surpassing consensus expectations, and sees ongoing strength in wireless and improving cable trends. In addition, Casey underscores the substantial, rising implied value of Rogers’ sports and media holdings, including the planned full ownership of MLSE, as a key strategic asset that could unlock further shareholder value over time.

Casey covers the Communication Services sector, focusing on stocks such as BCE, Rogers Communication, and Cineplex. According to TipRanks, Casey has an average return of 6.5% and a 57.99% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, TD Cowen also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a C$60.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific: Buy Rating Reiterated on Solid Core Growth and Emerging AI, Reshoring Catalysts
data.description:
William Blair analyst Matt Larew has maintained their bullish stance on TMO stock, giving a Buy rating on April 17.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Matt Larew has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Thermo Fisher’s ability to deliver results broadly in line with expectations while still generating upside at the earnings level. He highlights that core growth matched guidance and consensus, with particular strength in Lab Products and Pharma Services offsetting softer performance in other divisions.

Matt also points to management’s upcoming commentary on guidance, reshoring opportunities, and the company’s positioning in AI as important potential catalysts for future growth. In his view, clarity on segment trends, regional demand, and the pacing of growth into 2026 supports a constructive outlook, reinforcing the rationale for maintaining a positive stance on the shares.

According to TipRanks, Larew is an analyst with an average return of -1.6% and a 44.76% success rate. Larew covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Danaher, Thermo Fisher, and Twist Bioscience.
